game_name: Fire Emblem: Genealogy of the Holy War

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Rock-paper-scissors mechanic (lances > swords > axes > lances) becomes core tactical consideration throughout game
Fire, Lightning, Wind with opposing strengths; Light and Dark exist outside triangle with advantage over elemental types
Character conversations unlock story and tactical benefits; married/sibling pairs gain critical hit boosts when adjacent
High-ranking units provide stat boosts to all units within three tiles; boost scales with Leadership rank
All non-plot-critical characters permanently removed if defeated; creates persistent consequence tension throughout
Second generation units inherit stat values and personal skills influenced by parent attributes; creates long-term strategic planning
Seizing castles triggers neighboring deployments and objective shifts; map state evolves with player actions
Weapon repair, buying/selling items, arena battles available at safe locations; castle towns within maps offer same services

typical_relief_triggers: Returning to home base between chapters
Character relationships and conversations provide narrative breathing room
Optional arena battles offer controlled practice scenarios
typical_tension_triggers: Permanent character death mechanic creates constant loss risk
Dynamic objective changes when player seizes castles (neighboring reinforcements)
Leader death or home castle seizure forces mission restart
Encountering new weapon/magic triangle matchups requires tactical recalibration
Two-generation story structure with generational power shifts
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tactical with narrative-driven intensity peaks; tension maintained through permadeath system and dynamic map objectives; relief cycles occur between missions at home base

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Castle towns within mission maps provide optional services (repair, commerce) distinct from mandatory combat objectives

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of Weapon Triangle and basic tactical positioning; foundational relationship-building with safe story conversations
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Dynamic objective shifts triggered by player castle seizures; Introduction of Leadership rank mechanics; Generational transition introduces stat inheritance complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Second generation units with inherited attributes from first generation parental choices; accumulated permanent losses from fallen units; escalating magical triangle complexity alongside weapon triangle
notable_spikes: Transition to Generation 2 with new cast requiring different tactical approaches
Encounters with units possessing high Leadership ranks affecting large areas
Story-critical battles with scripted reinforcements tied to player progress

notable_level_patterns.description: Castle seizure triggers scripted changes—neighboring enemy castles deploy additional troops, shifting mission scope. Actions in mission's early phases alter later phases' enemy behavior.

Story divided across two generational progressions with same cast structure. First generation establishes relationships and attributes that mechanically influence second generation units' starting stats and personal skills.

Character conversations unlock optional story content and stat-boosting support effects. Marriages influence stat inheritance and create tactical bonuses (critical hit boosts when paired units are adjacent).
Non-plot-critical character deaths are permanent and mission-ending for the player's leader, creating escalating resource scarcity as game progresses and irreplaceable units are lost.
